questions: I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. I need to submit a routine status update on a project I've been working on for three months. How do I write it?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. The office printer is jammed and I need to print a document before my meeting in ten minutes. How do I deal with it?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. My landlord asks me to read over the lease renewal and sign it by Friday. How do I approach this?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. A friend texts asking for my opinion on two nearly identical couches they're considering buying. How do I respond?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. My doctor's office leaves a voicemail asking me to schedule a routine annual checkup. What do I do?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. I need to fill out a form for a new gym membership with my basic contact information. How do I go about it?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. I'm browsing my email inbox and see a newsletter I subscribed to months ago about local events. What do I do with it?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. My bus card is running low and I need to top it up before my commute tomorrow. How do I handle this?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. I get an automated notification that my credit card statement is ready to view. What do I do?
I stay detached and practical — things don't tend to pull me in. I need to reply to a routine meeting invitation for next Tuesday. How do I respond?
